edges.description: Constitutive NOD2 activation drives granuloma formation in joints, skin, and eyes.
Non-caseating granulomas form in the dermis.
Granulomatous inflammation extends to visceral organs and the vasculature.
Systemic granulomatous autoinflammation can include recurrent fever.
Granulomatous synovitis manifests clinically as polyarticular arthritis.
Chronic granulomatous tenosynovitis and joint involvement can present with camptodactyly.
intermediate_mechanisms: Chronic tenosynovitis and joint contracture limit finger extension.

edges.description: Ocular granulomatous inflammation manifests as uveitis/panuveitis.
Chronic uveitis and its complications produce visual impairment.
Cataract is a complication of chronic ocular inflammation.
Dermal granulomas manifest as the characteristic rash or dermatitis.
Expanded vascular involvement manifests as vasculitis.
Expanded visceral involvement can include interstitial lung disease.

nodes.description: Flexion contractures of fingers due to chronic granulomatous tenosynovitis, a characteristic feature of Blau syndrome joint involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Blau Syndrome: Challenging Molecular Genetic Diagnostics of Autoinflammatory Disease.
evidence.snippet: In two individuals from one family, only camptodactyly was noted, while another member had camptodactyly in combination with non-active uveitis and angioid streaks

nodes.description: Secondary cataracts develop as a complication of chronic granulomatous uveitis and/or prolonged corticosteroid therapy.

evidence.reference_title: Pediatric granulomatous arthritis: an international registry.
evidence.snippet: cataracts in 50% of patients

nodes.description: Skin involvement manifests as a symmetrical, tan-colored, papular or lichenoid rash, often the earliest clinical sign. Biopsy reveals non-caseating granulomas in the dermis.

evidence.reference_title: Pediatric granulomatous arthritis: an international registry.
evidence.snippet: Cutaneous presentation was the most common

nodes.description: Intermittent fevers may occur, particularly during disease flares, reflecting the systemic autoinflammatory nature of the condition.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term prognosis of 47 pediatric patients with Blau syndrome in China.
evidence.snippet: At baseline, incidence of fever, arthritis, rash, dermatitis and uveitis were 34%, 93.6%, 72.3% and 31.9%

evidence.reference_title: NOD2 gene-associated pediatric granulomatous arthritis: clinical diversity, novel and recurrent mutations, and evidence of clinical improvement with interleukin-1 blockade in a Spanish cohort.
evidence.snippet: Novel atypical manifestations such as persistent fever and myocardiopathy were also observed

nodes.description: Polyarticular boggy synovitis with non-caseating granulomas, typically affecting wrists, ankles, knees, and interphalangeal joints. Usually the presenting feature, with onset before age 4.

evidence.reference_title: Blau syndrome: cross-sectional data from a multicentre study of clinical, radiological and functional outcomes.
evidence.snippet: Arthritis, documented in all but one patient, was oligoarticular in 7, polyarticular in 23

evidence.reference_title: Long-term prognosis of 47 pediatric patients with Blau syndrome in China.
evidence.snippet: At baseline, incidence of fever, arthritis, rash, dermatitis and uveitis were 34%, 93.6%, 72.3% and 31.9%

evidence.reference_title: Pediatric granulomatous arthritis: an international registry.
evidence.snippet: Arthritis was polyarticular in 96% of patients

nodes.description: Symmetric, tan-colored or erythematous papular rash, often ichthyosiform or lichenoid in character. Usually the earliest clinical manifestation, appearing in infancy.

evidence.reference_title: Pediatric granulomatous arthritis: an international registry.
evidence.snippet: Cutaneous presentation was the most common

evidence.reference_title: Long-term prognosis of 47 pediatric patients with Blau syndrome in China.
evidence.snippet: At baseline, incidence of fever, arthritis, rash, dermatitis and uveitis were 34%, 93.6%, 72.3% and 31.9%

nodes.description: Constitutive NOD2 activation drives formation of non-caseating epithelioid granulomas in joints, skin, and uveal tract. Multinucleated giant cells and epithelioid macrophages accumulate, causing tissue damage and organ dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: NOD2 gene-associated pediatric granulomatous arthritis: clinical diversity, novel and recurrent mutations, and evidence of clinical improvement with interleukin-1 blockade in a Spanish cohort.
evidence.snippet: Blau syndrome and early-onset sarcoidosis are NOD2 gene-associated chronic autoinflammatory diseases characterized by skin rash, arthritis, and/or eye involvement, with noncaseating granulomata as their pathologic hallmark

nodes.description: Bilateral panuveitis with mutton-fat keratic precipitates, posterior synechiae, and risk of cataracts, glaucoma, and vision loss. Most sight-threatening complication.

evidence.reference_title: Blau syndrome: cross-sectional data from a multicentre study of clinical, radiological and functional outcomes.
evidence.snippet: Ocular disease was documented in 25 of 31 patients, with vitreous inflammation in 64% and moderate-severe visual loss in 33%

evidence.reference_title: Pediatric granulomatous arthritis: an international registry.
evidence.snippet: Isolated eye disease was never the presenting symptom, but significant/severe visual impairment was observed in 41% of patients

nodes.description: Granulomatous interstitial lung disease may occur as part of the expanded visceral manifestations of Blau syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term prognosis of 47 pediatric patients with Blau syndrome in China.
evidence.snippet: Incidence of vasculitis and interstitial lung disease were 27.7% and 17.0%, respectively

nodes.description: Granulomatous synovitis leads to progressive polyarthritis with boggy synovial swelling, loss of range of motion, and potential joint destruction. Wrists, ankles, knees, and small joints of hands are most commonly affected.

evidence.reference_title: Blau syndrome: cross-sectional data from a multicentre study of clinical, radiological and functional outcomes.
evidence.snippet: The most frequently involved joints at presentation were wrists, ankles, knees and PIPs

nodes.description: Leucine to phenylalanine substitution in the NOD/NACHT domain. Associated with typical Blau syndrome phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: CARD15 mutations in Blau syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We have identified three missense mutations in the nucleotide-binding domain (NBD) of CARD15/NOD2 in four French and German families with Blau syndrome

evidence.reference_title: CARD15 mutations in Blau syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We have identified three missense mutations in the nucleotide-binding domain (NBD) of CARD15/NOD2 in four French and German families with Blau syndrome

evidence.reference_title: Blau syndrome: cross-sectional data from a multicentre study of clinical, radiological and functional outcomes.
evidence.snippet: Of the 31 patients, 11 carried the p.R334W NOD2 mutation, 9 the p.R334Q and 11 various other NOD2 missense mutations; 20 patients were sporadic and 11 from five BS pedigrees

evidence.reference_title: NOD2 gene-associated pediatric granulomatous arthritis: clinical diversity, novel and recurrent mutations, and evidence of clinical improvement with interleukin-1 blockade in a Spanish cohort.
evidence.snippet: NOD2 analysis revealed 1 heterozygous mutation in each patient, and familial studies confirmed its full penetrance

evidence.reference_title: Distinct NOD2 mutations reported in three families with Blau syndrome (BS) from a single center in India - Case series and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: The first family had three affected members where the mother and her two children had skin changes, polyarthritis and a pathogenic mutation in NOD2 gene (exon 4, c.1000C > T, p.Arg334Trp) suggesting BS

evidence.reference_title: NOD2 / Blau syndrome (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: NOD2 | HGNC:5331 | Blau syndrome | MONDO:0008523 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Blau syndrome is caused by gain-of-function mutations in NOD2 (CARD15), predominantly in the nucleotide-binding oligomerization domain (NOD/NACHT). These mutations lead to constitutive activation of NF-kB signaling, resulting in excessive pro-inflammatory cytokine production and non-caseating granuloma formation in affected tissues.

evidence.reference_title: CARD15 mutations in Blau syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We have identified three missense mutations in the nucleotide-binding domain (NBD) of CARD15/NOD2 in four French and German families with Blau syndrome

evidence.reference_title: Novel NOD2 Mutation in Early-Onset Inflammatory Bowel Phenotype.
evidence.snippet: HEK293 cells transfected with the mutated cDNA showed a 20-fold increase of NF-kB activation in basal condition

evidence.reference_title: Role and molecular mechanism of NOD2 in chronic non-communicable diseases.
evidence.snippet: Gene mutations of NOD2 lead to the development of autoimmune diseases such as Crohn's disease and Blau syndrome

evidence.reference_title: Potential Benefits of TNF Targeting Therapy in Blau Syndrome, a NOD2-Associated Systemic Autoinflammatory Granulomatosis.
evidence.snippet: abnormal cytokine expression in macrophages from untreated patients requires IFNγ stimulation, and that anti-TNF treatment corrects the abnormalities associated with Blau syndrome, even in the presence of IFNγ

nodes.description: Granulomatous uveitis, typically panuveitis, is the most sight-threatening manifestation. Can progress to cataracts, glaucoma, band keratopathy, and blindness if untreated.

evidence.reference_title: Blau syndrome: cross-sectional data from a multicentre study of clinical, radiological and functional outcomes.
evidence.snippet: Ocular disease was documented in 25 of 31 patients, with vitreous inflammation in 64% and moderate-severe visual loss in 33%

evidence.reference_title: Pediatric granulomatous arthritis: an international registry.
evidence.snippet: Eye disease was bilateral in 21 of 22 patients and was complicated by glaucoma in 6 of 22 patients and by cataracts in 50% of patients

nodes.description: Arginine to glutamine substitution at the same position as R334W. Associated with classic Blau triad.

evidence.reference_title: Blau syndrome: cross-sectional data from a multicentre study of clinical, radiological and functional outcomes.
evidence.snippet: Of the 31 patients, 11 carried the p.R334W NOD2 mutation, 9 the p.R334Q and 11 various other NOD2 missense mutations

nodes.description: Most common Blau syndrome mutation. Arginine to tryptophan substitution in the NOD/NACHT domain leading to constitutive NF-kB activation.

evidence.reference_title: Blau syndrome: cross-sectional data from a multicentre study of clinical, radiological and functional outcomes.
evidence.snippet: Of the 31 patients, 11 carried the p.R334W NOD2 mutation, 9 the p.R334Q and 11 various other NOD2 missense mutations

nodes.description: Systemic vasculitis affecting large and medium vessels, representing an expanded manifestation beyond the classic triad.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term prognosis of 47 pediatric patients with Blau syndrome in China.
evidence.snippet: Incidence of vasculitis and interstitial lung disease were 27.7% and 17.0%, respectively

nodes.description: Beyond the classic triad, Blau syndrome can involve visceral organs (liver, kidney, lung) and vasculature. Interstitial lung disease and vasculitis represent expanded manifestations that may be life-threatening.

evidence.reference_title: Blau syndrome: cross-sectional data from a multicentre study of clinical, radiological and functional outcomes.
evidence.snippet: Expanded manifestations (visceral, vascular) beyond the classic clinical triad were seen in 52%

evidence.reference_title: Long-term prognosis of 47 pediatric patients with Blau syndrome in China.
evidence.snippet: Incidence of vasculitis and interstitial lung disease were 27.7% and 17.0%, respectively

nodes.description: Progressive visual loss due to complications of chronic uveitis including cataracts, glaucoma, band keratopathy, and macular edema.

evidence.reference_title: Pediatric granulomatous arthritis: an international registry.
evidence.snippet: significant/severe visual impairment was observed in 41% of patients
